attributes.description: Alerts when `haproxy.requests.average_time > 10 ms`. Any non-zero queue time signals backend saturation.
attributes.name: [HAProxy OTel] High backend queue time

investigation_guide.blob:
## HAProxy high backend queue time

### What fired
`haproxy.requests.average_time > 10 ms` during the window.

### Why it matters
Queue time is how long requests wait for a backend slot. Non-zero values mean all configured connections on at least one backend server are saturated.

### Triage
1. Check session utilisation on the affected backend.
2. Look at server max-conn and active connection counts.
3. Correlate with upstream server-side latency.

### Remediation
- Raise per-server max-conn.
- Add servers to the backend.

### Tuning
- 10 ms is a sensitive default; raise for bursty workloads.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-haproxyreceiver.otel-*
// Queue time (wait for server assignment) — any non-zero signals saturation
| WHERE haproxy.requests.average_time IS NOT NULL
| STATS max_qtime = MAX(haproxy.requests.average_time)
  BY haproxy.proxy_name, haproxy.service_name, haproxy.addr
// Threshold in ms — even small values indicate capacity pressure
| WHERE max_qtime > 10
| EVAL alert_key = CONCAT(TO_STRING(haproxy.proxy_name), "::", TO_STRING(haproxy.service_name), "::", COALESCE(TO_STRING(haproxy.addr), ""))
| SORT max_qtime DESC
| LIMIT 50
